records.board: Alaska Board of Social Work Examiners
requirementSummary: Second or subsequent biennial renewal: 45 contact hours. First biennial renewal: 21 hours if licensed fewer than 12 months or 45 hours if licensed at least 12 months. Minimum categories each biennium: 6 substance abuse, 6 cross-cultural education, 3 professional ethics, 3 teletherapy practice, and 3 suicide education. At least 10 hours must be live, live online, or synchronous. Clinical supervisors must complete an additional 6 supervision hours for licensing periods beginning after June 30, 2026.

records.board: Alaska Board of Marital and Family Therapy
requirementSummary: License expires December 31 of even-numbered years. If licensed 18 months or more in the concluding period: 45 contact hours, including at least 2 hours professional ethics, 2 addictions, 2 cross-cultural education, and 2 domestic violence. If participating in teletherapy, 2 hours in teletherapy practice including teletherapy ethics. Supervisors must complete 2 supervision-related hours, which may count toward the total. Reduced totals apply to first renewal if licensed for less than 18 months.

categories.summary: License expires December 31 of even-numbered years. Reduced totals apply to first renewal if licensed for less than 18 months.

records.board: Alaska Board of Professional Counselors
requirementSummary: Second or subsequent biennial renewal: 40 contact hours. First biennial renewal varies by time licensed: 20 hours if licensed fewer than 12 months, 30 hours if 12 to fewer than 18 months, 40 hours if 18 months or more. For biennial periods beginning on or after November 1, 2023: at least 3 hours professional ethics, 3 hours suicidality, and 3 hours cultural competency. Minimum 20 hours must be synchronous; no more than one-half may be individual study.

categories.summary: Second or subsequent biennial renewal: 40 contact hours. Minimum 20 hours must be synchronous; no more than one-half may be individual study.

records.board: Arkansas Board of Examiners in Counseling
requirementSummary: All licenses expire biennially on May 31 of the renewal year. Renewal requires at least 24 clock hours of continuing professional education/training in the 24 months before renewal, including at least 3 clock hours in ethics relevant to the license being renewed. Remaining hours must be marriage and family therapy/counseling in nature and support the licensee's Statement of Intent. Dual LAC/LPC and LAMFT/LMFT licensees still require 24 hours with 3 ethics, with the remaining 21 balanced between counseling and MFT content. Board-approved supervisors need 6 supervision hours each renewal period, with 3 from Board-recognized state associations or an Arkansas university-associated workshop/program.

requirementSummary: Each marital and family therapist applying for renewal must complete at least 15 hours of qualifying continuing education during the one-year period for which the license has been renewed. For registration periods ending after January 1, 2016, at least 2 contact hours on mental health conditions common to veterans and family members of veterans are required at first applicable renewal and at least once every 6 years thereafter. Certificates must be retained for at least 3 years.
